name: Brown Rice Puttu with Green Gram
source_note: Resources/Recipes/Breakfast/Brown Rice Puttu with Green Gram.md
description: The recipe uses gentle seasoning and controlled oil so the grain, lentil, and vegetable flavors stay clear and clean. Brown Rice Puttu with Green Gram brings a distinct structure, seasoning pattern, and grain-legume balance to the table.
cooking_steps: Fill the puttu cylinder in alternating layers of grated coconut, damp flour, and cooked green gram, finishing with a spoon of coconut.
Steam over medium heat for 6 to 8 minutes until a strong column of steam escapes from the top and the puttu smells fully cooked.
Rest 1 minute, then push out gently onto a plate so the cylinder stays intact.
Serve warm, breaking it up lightly with a fork before eating.
meal_prep_notes: Puttu is best fresh but can be refrigerated for 1 day. Reheat by steaming for 3 minutes. For batch prep, pre-moisten the flour and hold it refrigerated in an airtight box, then steam portions to order.
